question: Does the image depict a technical drawing consisting of exactly three distinct orthographic views?
Is the Front View positioned at the upper left of the layout?
Is the Side View positioned at the upper right of the layout?
Is the Top View positioned at the lower left of the layout (directly below the Front View)?
Are the Front View and Side View aligned horizontally, and the Front View and Top View aligned vertically?
Does the geometric subject depicted resemble a regular hexagonal prism (six-sided base)?
Is one corner of the hexagonal prism visually truncated (cut off by an oblique plane)?
Are the views connected by projection alignment lines?
Are the projection alignment lines styled as dash-dot center lines?
Are hidden edges within the views represented by dashed lines?
Are numerical dimension annotations (arrows and numbers) present on the drawing?
Is the specific text 'Scale 1:2' present in the image?
Is the 'Scale 1:2' text located in the lower right corner of the image?
